DENUNCIAN “PRECIOS PERSONALIZADOS” EN COMPRAS ONLINE.

Usuarios en redes sociales comenzaron a compartir capturas que muestran el mismo producto con precios distintos según quién lo mire dentro de Mercado Libre, lo que reavivó el debate sobre los llamados precios dinámicos o personalizados en el comercio electrónico.

El economista Ariel Setton, especializado en medios de pago, señala: "Está implementado desde octubre del año pasado. Los vendedores pueden poner un rango máximo y mínimo, ponen unos requisitos y dependiendo de quién abra puede ver hasta el doble del precio".

"Los vendedores tienen más alternativas para sacar el mayor provecho de los precios a los cuales compran".

"Si uno comparte una publicación de Mercado Libre aclare cuál es el precio, porque depende de quién lo abre", agrega.

"Seguramente la compañía tendrá más detalles del algoritmo. No tiene que ver con el poder económico, tampoco tiene que ver con la cantidad de veces que el consumidor entra o compra en Mercado Libre", sostiene Setton.

"Amazon lo hace hace 14 años: claramente esto tiene beneficios para la empresa".

The market frequently evaluates $MELI's ecom and fintech segments independently, and heavily penalizes the stock when margins in a specific area such as credit or logistics experience compression.

The internal growth strategy is explicitly focused on optimizing for the global max value rather than the local max value in each vertical. There is a deliberate willingness to lower profitability in one vertical such as consumer credit to aggressively expand the top of funnel and cross sell other products such as insurance, retail, and digital accounts. By subsidizing one arm of the business, the overall pie expands.

Skeptics view a few quarters of margin compression as a fundamental headwind or a sign of competitive pricing pressure. Internally, this is viewed as a calculated customer acquisition cost. This ecosystem arbitrage locks users into a high switching cost environment that standalone competitors whether pure-play ecom or pure-play digital banks cannot replicate.

Let's also keep in mind that these verticals require years of capital intense building, regulatory approvals, and building customer trust. The further I look into MELI, the deeper and more embeded I see the ecosystem is becoming in LatAm.

I see a lot of people talking about increasing their cash positions and essentially trying to predict a recession. This could very well work. I cannot predict anything but I personally stay fully invested in the market. I can look back on countless of times where I wish I was more patient when buying dips but it does not really affect my overall strategy. I own durable strong businesses that would come out stronger from a recession like $MELI or $AMZN. That does not mean the sock price would suffer in the meantime but on a multi-year horizon it doesn’t really matter whether I entered now or 30%+ lower from here. The bigger risk is trying to time the market and risking not having exposure imo. There are a lot of different philosophies that work but if a business looks like a great deal right now and on a forward basis, I don’t see why I should hold off on owning it. Maybe its different for folks who do not have an income. Everybody may prefer a different style. I just seen more people get hurt by trying to time the market than holding strong businesses.
